core_data_or_observation: 1794 current private unicorns; USD 1.52T raised; USD 10T reported value; valuations based on most recently disclosed funding rounds
reliability: Tier 1 for reported private-market board data
kolm_implication: Treat unicorn status as a broad market label; compete on durable product quality, not valuation optics

core_data_or_observation: USD 300B global venture investment in Q1 2026 across 6000 startups; AI received USD 242B or 80%; four major AI/autonomy rounds represented USD 188B or 65%
reliability: Tier 1 for Crunchbase reported funding data
kolm_implication: AI buyers see heavily funded, polished vendors; Kolm must show concrete product surfaces, control objects, and proof

core_data_or_observation: US and Canadian startups raised USD 252.6B in Q1; more than 87% went to AI-related categories; USD 222.4B or 88% went to late-stage and technology-growth rounds
reliability: Tier 1 for Crunchbase reported funding data
kolm_implication: The market is concentrated in perceived winners; Kolm should optimize for precision and leverage, not broad platform sprawl

source: Axios summary of PitchBook undercorn research
core_data_or_observation: More than one-quarter of VC-backed unicorns may fall below USD 1B under PitchBook mark-to-market; top 10 account for roughly 52% of aggregate value
kolm_implication: Avoid public valuation language; anchor claims to usage, controls, routes, artifacts, receipts, tests, and readiness gates

source: TechCrunch Aileen Lee unicorn origin piece
core_data_or_observation: Original unicorn analysis found 39 US software companies started since 2003; estimated about 0.07% of venture-backed consumer and enterprise software startups; enterprise unicorns had strong value per private dollar
kolm_implication: Unicorns moved from rare label to crowded category; durable enterprise value remains tied to capital efficiency and deep workflow ownership

core_data_or_observation: 30 prominent agents annotated across 45 fields; only 4 of 13 frontier-autonomy agents disclosed agentic safety evaluations; 25 of 30 disclosed no internal safety results; 23 of 30 had no third-party testing
reliability: Tier 1/2 research index with transparent methodology
kolm_implication: Kolm can differentiate by making evaluation, receipts, lineage, and readiness gates visible instead of hiding agent risk

core_data_or_observation: Enterprise page exposes model access, MCP controls, system-level agent rules, SAML SSO, SCIM, centralized controls, usage/cost limits, analytics exports, zero data retention language, trust center
kolm_implication: Kolm account control center must expose model/provider controls, connector controls, permissions, usage, export, and audit as first-class objects

core_data_or_observation: Databricks frames AI around data-connected agents, custom evaluation, governance, guardrails, access controls, rate limits, lineage, model serving, MLflow, Unity Catalog, and AI Gateway
kolm_implication: Kolm should not be generic AI; it must own behavior-to-artifact lifecycle with evaluation gates and governance exports

core_data_or_observation: Glean emphasizes connectors, actions, model hub, APIs, security, MCP gateway, permissions, observability, governance, company context, work execution, and tools where employees already work
kolm_implication: Kolm needs broad ingress/egress coverage and permission-aware enterprise context without becoming generic workplace search

core_data_or_observation: Harvey presents assistant, vault, knowledge, workflow agents, shared spaces, enterprise controls, SAML SSO, audit logs, IP allow-listing, data lifecycle management, and security/compliance posture
kolm_implication: High-stakes vertical AI buyers expect workflow depth and procurement-grade controls by default

core_data_or_observation: Ramp shows spend controls, policy enforcement, roles, workflow builder, approvals, purchase tracking, integrations, APIs, ERP sync, multiple entities, and operations automation
kolm_implication: Best unicorn pages show the controlled business object and workflow states; Kolm pages should expose sources, traces, policies, artifacts, receipts, exports, and gates

core_data_or_observation: TRAC uses 30+ data sources; top-investor signal eliminates most startups; reported investor demand can exceed round sizes by 10x; five companies crossed USD 1B while article was in progress
kolm_implication: Future-unicorn formation is faster and more competitive; Kolm needs category clarity before growth messaging

core_data_or_observation: Business page presents API platform, agents, app integrations, enterprise-grade security, admin controls, SAML SSO, privacy, retention, and compliance posture
kolm_implication: Kolm public pages must expose API paths, admin controls, privacy posture, and evidence-backed trust instead of generic AI claims

source: Anthropic Claude pricing and enterprise flow
core_data_or_observation: Enterprise recommendation flow asks about team size, security and compliance needs, usage, contract type, SCIM, audit logs, access controls, HIPAA-ready needs, and tailored contracts
kolm_implication: Kolm enterprise signup and control center should route buyers by security posture, usage shape, contract needs, and admin-control requirements

core_data_or_observation: AI Gateway provides one key across hundreds of models, unified API, retries/fallbacks, embeddings, spend monitoring, BYOK, and OpenAI/Anthropic compatibility
kolm_implication: Kolm should match gateway ergonomics but differentiate through capture, eval gates, artifacts, receipts, runtime targets, and governance exports

core_data_or_observation: API docs emphasize resource-oriented URLs, standard verbs/status codes, JSON responses, authentication, sandboxes, versioning, and object-per-request semantics
kolm_implication: Kolm APIs need predictable resource contracts, sandbox/test behavior, versioning, idempotency, clear errors, and examples for each product object

core_data_or_observation: Docs expose database, auth, storage, realtime, edge functions, REST API, GraphQL API, SDKs, management API, migration guides, integrations, and self-hosting paths
kolm_implication: Kolm docs should support API, SDK, CLI, migration/import, management, integration, and BYOC/self-hosted-style paths

core_data_or_observation: Pioneer Agent automates closed-loop small-language-model improvement through data curation, failure diagnosis, regression avoidance, retraining, and verification
reliability: Tier 1 research paper for described method/results
kolm_implication: Kolm should beat Pioneer by adding enterprise policy, behavior capture, portable artifacts, receipts, runtime targets, and governance exports around the improvement loop

core_data_or_observation: Proposes search, resolve, preview, execute, verify, recover semantics plus normalized tool contracts, evidence chains, capability policies, and risk escalation for enterprise agent systems
reliability: Tier 2 emerging research because claims are paper-reported
kolm_implication: Kolm API/control-center design should treat agent/API actions as governed state transitions with evidence and recovery, not only CRUD logs

core_data_or_observation: Frames enterprise AI as orchestration of agents, proprietary data, registries, streams, planners, and QoS requirements such as cost, accuracy, and latency
kolm_implication: Kolm should expose registries, source metadata, streams, planner/eval state, and cost/quality/latency controls as product objects

core_data_or_observation: Developer surface exposes MCP setup across coding tools, client APIs, indexing APIs, OpenAPI specs, governance, verification, permission-aware access, and 100+ app connector positioning
kolm_implication: Kolm docs should expose MCP/tool traces, OpenAPI, verifier examples, governance objects, indexing/import APIs, and permission-aware connector semantics

core_data_or_observation: Stripe documents idempotency keys for safely retrying state-changing requests, parameter comparison on retries, key pruning behavior, and POST-only application
kolm_implication: Kolm should define idempotency for compile runs, exports, release promotion, webhook delivery, and any state-changing enterprise route

core_data_or_observation: Studies two-timescale self-evolution for frozen SLM agents through low-risk prompt refinement and higher-risk control-logic updates accepted through held-out validation
kolm_implication: Kolm artifact lifecycle should store prompt/control changes, acceptance gates, rejected updates, holdout status, and rollback targets

core_data_or_observation: Frames compact-model policy repair through experience abstraction, failure analysis, small auditable policy patches, and conservative checks
kolm_implication: Kolm should treat policy repair and improvement steps as auditable artifacts tied to failures, evals, receipts, and readiness gates

core_data_or_observation: Profiles AI unicorns with very small teams and high valuation or revenue per employee, including coding, robotics, model, and research companies
kolm_implication: Do not equate headcount with product maturity; Kolm should optimize for leverage but keep enterprise controls and evidence strong enough for procurement

core_data_or_observation: AI Gateway docs emphasize universal API access across supported models, simple and semantic cache, MCP support, fallbacks, advanced routing, and integrated guardrails
kolm_implication: Routing, fallbacks, and guardrails are table stakes; Kolm must differentiate through artifact compilation, receipts, target readiness, and governance exports

core_data_or_observation: Docs expose tracing, prompt management, production and development evaluations, datasets, human annotations, custom scores, SDKs, framework support, and multimodal tracing
kolm_implication: Kolm should import traces and evals from tools like Langfuse, then turn evaluated behavior into signed artifacts and exportable proof

core_data_or_observation: Docs organize the workflow around tracing, logs, human review, labels and corrections, datasets, exports, evaluations, and build/evaluate/improve loops
kolm_implication: Kolm should ingest Braintrust-like data and close the loop from failure to artifact instead of remaining an analysis dashboard

core_data_or_observation: Docs expose offline inference, online serving, OpenAI-compatible server, tool calling, structured outputs, observability, production metrics, deployment options, security, and integrations
kolm_implication: Kolm runtime readiness must be target-specific, with proof states and export recipes instead of blanket deployment claims

core_data_or_observation: MCP is presented as an open standard for connecting AI applications to external systems, tools, data sources, and workflows with broad ecosystem support
kolm_implication: Kolm should support MCP as ingress and egress for agent/tool traces, artifact controls, and callable signed behaviors where appropriate

core_data_or_observation: Argues enterprise AI governance should use continuous telemetry, proactive discovery, automated probes, and architecture-backed proof rather than point-in-time manual attestation
kolm_implication: Kolm should make evidence over attestation a product principle and tie governance claims to traces, policies, receipts, and exports

core_data_or_observation: Identifies the observe-but-do-not-act gap in agent observability and proposes governance telemetry, policy detection, enforcement bus, and cryptographic provenance
kolm_implication: Kolm should connect telemetry to policy verdicts, enforcement handoffs, signed provenance, and artifact receipts

core_data_or_observation: Argues agentic systems need richer authorization semantics including inherited and delegated authority, time-limited authority, recursive delegation, and contextual scopes
kolm_implication: Kolm API Control Center should model agent scopes and delegated authority as first-class audit and policy objects

core_data_or_observation: Frames Europe's tech ecosystem around scaling friction, funding gaps, strategic sectors such as AI, security, data centers, semiconductors, energy, and defense
kolm_implication: Regional unicorn strategy depends on scaling capital and strategic infrastructure; Kolm should position around enterprise-grade control and exportable proof
